subject: Cloud Computing
title: Spot Instances: Bargain Computing
description: A quick win: understand Spot Instances: Bargain Computing.

hook.text: Cloud providers have spare servers sitting idle. They sell them at a steep discount — with a catch.

buildup.text: Spot instances cost up to 90% less than regular pricing. The trade-off: they can vanish any time.

discovery.text: When demand rises, the provider reclaims spot capacity. Your workload gets a two-minute warning.

twist.text: Companies save millions using spot instances for batch jobs, rendering, and testing — interruptible work.

climax.text: Spot pricing turns idle servers into revenue. It's the cloud's version of standby airline tickets.

punchline.text: Cheap cloud exists. You just can't count on it staying.

quiz.question: What is the key trade-off of using spot instances?
options: They are much cheaper but can be terminated by the provider at any time
They are free but limited to 10 minutes of runtime
They run faster but cannot access the internet
